retry_reason: The refcount operation always executes unconditionally; no failure path exists, so retry is not applicable.
contract_summary: A safe put wrapper for drm_connector objects. It decrements the reference count while holding the connector_list_lock. When the refcount reaches zero, the connector is added to a free list and a work item is scheduled to perform actual deallocation. The caller must have previously acquired a reference (e.g., via drm_connector_get) and must not access the connector after this call. The deferred free ensures the object remains valid until the scheduled work runs.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to decrement the refcount (put) and, if it reaches zero, schedule deferred destruction. This is a pure reference-count management wrapper with only the necessary cleanup logic.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test(&conn->base.refcount.refcount) decrements the refcount on conn. The access path conn.base.refcount.refcount.refs correctly traces from the parameter to the atomic_t field, and the type chain drm_connector--drm_mode_object--kref--refcount_t--atomic_t matches the struct definitions.
